data.description: When you make a standing jump, you can jump half your listed jump distance. Either foot can clear the ground on the jump, and it costs a full foot of movement. In some circumstances, a DM might allow you to make a Strength (Athletics) check to jump higher than normally possible. You can extend your arms above your head during a jump, and thus can reach a distance equal to 1.5 times your height.

You can move up to your speed in the specified units (feet, miles). At a normal pace, you can travel 300 feet in 6 seconds, 3 miles in 10 minutes, or 24 miles in 4 hours. When moving at a slow pace, you can cover 200 feet in 6 seconds, 2 miles in 10 minutes, or 18 miles in 4 hours. Moving at a slow pace also allows you to use the Stealth skill without being disadvantaged.

often But dense rubble-filled steep deep forests, swamps, mountains, ice-covered and difficult terrain. Voumove half in terrain-moving at speed difficult 1foot difficult terrain costs 2 of feet speed-so you in can only the distance in minute. cover half normal an hour, or day.

While climbing, swimming, or crawling, foot movement costs extra (2 feet) in difficult terrain. At a slow pace, characters can move 1/4 their speed. While traveling stealthily, they require a successful Stealth check as long as they're not in open terrain with few handholds.
